data.title: CME posts second-highest September and Q3 ADV
data.description:
CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) September average daily volume of 26.1M fell 8% from a year ago, and Q3 average daily volume of 25.3M declined 10% from Q3 2024, the company said on Thursday. Still, ADV for both periods represented the second-highest levels for any September or Q3 on record.

The quarter's performance included a record for cryptocurrency trading, at 340K contracts ADV with a notional value of $14.1B. Micro ether futures ADV hit a record of 209.0K contracts, and ether futures ADV reached 236K contracts.

September interest rate ADV of 13.3M contracts dropped 10% Y/Y.  Metals ADV surged 42% Y/Y to 1.00M contracts, with micro gold futures ADV surging 214% to 347K contracts.

Energy ADV of 2.26M contracts dropped 17% from a year ago, while FX ADV of 1.09M contracts fell 15%.

For the month, crypto ADV of 309K contracts represented $13.d7B notional amount. Micro ether futures ADV jumped 472% to 182K contracts, and micro bitcoin futures climbed 50% to 62K.

data.title: Brown Forman announces $400M stock buyback
data.description:
• Brown-Forman (BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B) Board of Directors approved a $400 million share repurchase authorization, commencing October 1, 2025, through October 1, 2026.
  • “Despite an increasingly competitive environment, we remain confident in our ability to generate strong cash flow,” said Brown-Forman President and CEO, Lawson Whiting. “This repurchase is aligned with our broader capital deployment priorities and reflects our commitment to returning capital to shareholders while maintaining flexibility to invest in growth.”

data.title: Paramount Resources declares CAD 0.05 dividend
data.description:
• Paramount Resources  (TSX:POU:CA) declares CAD 0.05/share monthly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Oct. 31; for shareholders of record Oct. 15; ex-div Oct. 15.
  • See POU:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: DoorDash completes Deliveroo acquisition
data.description:
DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) has completed its ~£2.9B acquisition of London-based Deliveroo after the boards of both companies agreed to the terms of a final cash offer.

The European Commission’s regulatory agency recently found that the combination of two delivery firms would not hamper competition given the availability of alternatives such as Uber Eats (UBER), Just Eat Takeaway (OTCPK:JTKWY), and other regional delivery platforms.

Under the terms of the deal, Deliveroo shareholders would receive 180 pence in cash per Deliveroo share, representing a ~44% premium to the closing price of Deliveroo stock on the last business day prior to DoorDash’s offer.

“I could not be more excited by the prospect of what DoorDash and Deliveroo will be able to accomplish together. We’ll cover more than 40 countries with a combined population of more than 1 billion people, enabling us to provide more local businesses with the tools and technology they need to thrive,” said DoorDash co-founder and CEO Tony Xu.

data.title: BioCardia regains Nasdaq compliance after balance sheet improvements; shares up
data.description:
• BioCardia (NASDAQ:BCDA) has regained compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements.
  • On October 1, 2025, Nasdaq confirmed BioCardia now meets the Equity Rule.
  • Compliance follows efforts to strengthen the company’s balance sheet, including raising new capital.
  • BCDA shares up 7% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Nuvini Group announces 10-to-1 reverse stock split; shares down 37% premarket
data.description:
• Nuvini Group Limited (NASDAQ:NVNI) will execute a 10-to-1 reverse stock split starting October 6, 2025.
  • Every 10 existing shares will convert into 1 new share, reducing total shares from ~100.3M to ~10M.
  • NVNI shares down 37% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Enphase and Essent launch smart battery program in Netherlands
data.description:
• Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) is partnering with Dutch energy provider Essent to offer IQ Battery integration for solar customers.
  • The Smart Steering program allows Enphase users to earn up to €122/month by letting Essent remotely manage their home battery.
  • Compensation scales with battery size, up to 20 kWh, under a fixed-rate energy contract.
  • The program helps maximize solar self-consumption, reduce energy bills, and support grid reliability.
  • This initiative comes as the Netherlands plans to phase out net energy metering by 2027.
  • Enphase's IQ Battery 5P systems support 5–60 kWh setups and come with a 15-year warranty in the Netherlands.
  • The rollout begins with a limited group of Enphase solar-only customers, with wider access planned.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: REA Group takes controlling stake in Canadian PropTech firm Planitar (iGUIDE) to boost 3D offerings
The9-backed Nanyang Biologics to go public via $1.5B SPAC merger; shares up
data.description:
• Nanyang Biologics, backed by The9 Limited (NASDAQ:NCTY), will merge with SPAC RF Acquisition Corp II (NASDAQ:RFAI) to become publicly listed on Nasdaq.
  • The combined company will trade under the reserved ticker symbol “NYB” after closing.
  • The deal values NYB at a $1.5B pre-transaction equity value.
  • NYB shareholders, including The9, will roll over 100% of their equity and retain majority ownership and board control.
  • The transaction has board approval and is expected to close in Q1 or Q2 2026.
  • NYB will leverage its AI platform to speed up drug discovery and develop natural ingredient-based therapies.
  • NCTY shares up 13% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Popeyes expanding across Mexico with pipeline of 300+ new restaurants
data.description:
• Restaurant Brands International’s (NYSE:QSR) Popeyes chicken franchise is planning a rapid expansion across Mexico by partnering with regional franchisees, including long-standing Popeyes developer, Star Louisiana.
  • The company plans to open more than 300 Popeyes restaurants over the next 10 years across the Northwest, West, Central, and Southeast regions of Mexico. 
  • “Mexico represents a tremendous growth opportunity for Popeyes, and we're thrilled to be partnering with experienced and passionate franchisees who share our vision," said Restaurant Brands’ president Duncan Montero.  
  • By expanding across Mexico, Restaurant Brands (NYSE:QSR) aims to capitalize on one of the company’s fastest growing demographics, and Popeyes’ international growth momentum.
  • Besides Popeyes, Restaurant Brands (NYSE:QSR) is also the parent company of Burger King, Firehouse Subs, and Tim Hortons.
